title: sapium haitiense
intro: Sapium haitiense, also known as the Haitian stinking nut, is a plant species native to Haiti and the Dominican Republic. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Sapium haitiense include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Sapium haitiense are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
